perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date', the contract creates an in-text contradiction because there is no time limit now, it changes the scope of the effectivity of the legal document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the 'two (2) years from the Effective Date' clause, meaning the agreement now has no defined end date unless terminated by either party. This creates a contradiction because, without a specific duration, the agreement could theoretically last indefinitely, which conflicts with the implied understanding of a contract having a defined lifespan. The Perturbation Explanation states that this changes the scope of effectivity of the legal document, which is a contradiction. The Omissions - In Text Contradiction Definition specifies that omissions can create contradictions with constraints stated in other sections.
